docs.title: User Guide for BORIS App, the Behavioral Observation Research Interactive Software
docs.text:
BORIS App is a user-friendly application designed for live observations with Android. It is free and open source, and it runs on Android.

The official BORIS website is https://www.boris.unito.it.

This user guide applies to BORIS App version 0.13.

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
Friard, O. and Gamba, M.,
BORIS: a free, versatile open-source event-logging software for video/audio coding and live observations.
(2016) Methods in Ecology and Evolution, 7: 1325-1330.

DOI: 10.1111/2041-210X.12584

docs.title: Acknowledgement
docs.text: The authors would like to thank all users who report bugs and request new features for their valuable help.

docs.title: Citing BORIS
docs.text:
If you use BORIS in a publication, please cite:

Friard, O. and Gamba, M.,
BORIS: a free, versatile open-source event-logging software for video/audio coding and live observations.
(2016) Methods in Ecology and Evolution, 7: 1325-1330.

DOI: 10.1111/2041-210X.12584

You can also send us a nice postcard.

Please consider giving a star to the BORIS App GitHub repository.

docs.title: Bug Reports and Feature Requests
docs.text:
Please report any bugs you find in the latest BORIS version through the BORIS App GitHub repository.

Before reporting a bug, please:

  • Check the Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) section.
  • Check whether the issue has already been reported on GitHub.

Remember to indicate:

  • your operating system
  • your operating system version
  • the BORIS App version you are using

Provide all the information needed to reproduce the bug, such as a detailed procedure or a screen recording.

Note

If the bug you reported is fixed, please remember to close the issue.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
• Download the last version of the APK file from the GitHub repository release page:

https://github.com/olivierfriard/BORIS-App/releases

  • Follow How to Install APK on Android to install

docs.title: Starting BORIS
docs.text:
Once BORIS App is installed, it can be launched by taping on its icon.

The BORIS main window will appear. At this stage, all toolbar commands are disabled except the Preferences button.

The BORIS App main screen

docs.title: Open a project
docs.text:
• Press the Open project button

A list of available BORIS project files will open

The list of BORIS projects
  • Select a file and press the Open project button

BORIS App will show a summary of the selected project:

Details of the project

docs.title: Start a new observation
docs.text:
• Press the New observation button
New observation

  • Input an Observation id (mandatory, this id must be unique in your project)

  • Change the date (optional, default: current date time)

  • Input a description for your observation (optional)

  • If independent variables are defined, click on the Independent var button and fill the value for each variable.

The independent variables defined in the project
  • Press the Start observation button

You will obtain a screen with buttons corresponding to behaviors defined in your project. You can press it to code behaviors. The event time will be recorded in your observation.

The running observation

If behavioral categories are defined in your project, the behaviors will be grouped by category and buttons will be colored.

docs.title: Select the focal subject
docs.text:
• Press the Select focal subject button

  • Select the focal subject. If the focal subject is already selected, the subject will be deselected.

Select the focal subject

The focal subject will be show in the green button (at left bottom).

The running observation with a subject selected

docs.title: State events
docs.text:
If you press on a state event, the corresponding behavior button will be highlighted in red until you press it again to stop the state event.

A state event

docs.title: Modifiers selection
docs.text:
If modifiers are defined for the triggered behavior, BORIS App will show the modifiers page.

They are 3 types of modifiers:

  • Single item selection from a list

  • Multiple items selection from a list

  • Numerical

Various sets of modifiers can be defined for a behavior.

BORIS App will show a page with all sets of modifiers defined for the current behavior.

docs.title: Example for one set of modifiers (single item).
Example for one set of modifiers (multiple items). 2 modifiers are selected.
Example for 2 sets of modifiers (single item)
Stop the observation
docs.text:
• Press the Stop observation red button. Confirm that you want to stop the observation.

The observation will be saved in the current project.

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
These limitations should be fixed in next releases.

  • BORIS App can not handle independent variables defined as set of values
